017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.mcp;
018
019import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.bean.mcp.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.http.Content;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
027
028/**
029 * Drop-in {@link BasicRestServlet} subclass that exposes a single MCP JSON-RPC endpoint at {@code POST /}.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Subclasses provide their {@link McpServerConfig} by overriding {@link #createMcpConfig()} (called once at
033 * {@link #init() init} time), or by registering an {@link McpServerConfig} bean in their {@code RestContext}
034 * bean store.
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
037 * <pre>
038 * @Rest(path="/mcp")
039 * public class MyMcpServlet extends McpRestServlet {
040 *     @Override
041 *     protected McpServerConfig createMcpConfig() {
042 *         return new McpServerConfig()
043 *             .setServerInfo(new Implementation().setName("my-server").setVersion("1.0.0"))
044 *             .addTool(new MyEchoTool());
045 *     }
046 * }
047 * </pre>
048 *
049 * <p>
050 * The servlet enables {@code addBeanTypes} on its serializer so {@link Content} and {@link ResourceContents}
051 * polymorphic types are tagged with their {@code type} discriminator on the wire.
052 *
053 * @serial exclude
054 */
055@Rest
056@SerializerConfig(addBeanTypes = "true")
057public abstract class McpRestServlet extends BasicRestServlet {
058   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
059
060   private final transient AtomicReference<McpServerConfig> config = new AtomicReference<>();
061
062   /**
063    * Returns the {@link McpServerConfig} backing this servlet.
064    *
065    * <p>
066    * The default implementation lazily caches the value of {@link #createMcpConfig()}; subclasses needing
067    * per-request reconfiguration may override.
068    *
069    * @return The config. Never {@code null}.
070    */
071   public synchronized McpServerConfig getMcpConfig() {
072      var c = config.get();
073      if (c == null) {
074         c = createMcpConfig();
075         if (c == null)
076            throw new IllegalStateException("createMcpConfig() returned null");
077         config.set(c);
078      }
079      return c;
080   }
081
082   /**
083    * Factory method for the {@link McpServerConfig} backing this servlet.
084    *
085    * <p>
086    * Subclasses must override this method to declare their tools, prompts, and resources.
087    *
088    * @return A non-{@code null} server config.
089    */
090   protected abstract McpServerConfig createMcpConfig();
091
092   /**
093    * MCP JSON-RPC endpoint.
094    *
095    * @param req The parsed JSON-RPC request envelope.
096    * @param restReq The HTTP request (used to surface the bean store to handlers).
097    * @return The JSON-RPC response, or {@code null} for notifications (which the framework renders as an
098    *         empty body).
099    */
100   @SuppressWarnings({
101      "resource" // Request-scoped scratch BasicBeanStore; lifetime is bounded by this handler invocation, no foreign resources are captured.
102   })
103   @RestPost(path = "/")
104   public JsonRpcResponse handleMcp(@Content JsonRpcRequest req, RestRequest restReq) {
105      var bs = new BasicBeanStore(restReq.getContext().getBeanStore())
106         .addBean(RestRequest.class, restReq);
107      return Mcp.handle(req, getMcpConfig(), bs);
108   }
109}
